New anti-gravity update is insane. Google just dropped something that changes how AI works forever. It's called anti-gravity. And no, it's not just another coding tool. This thing gives AI agents actual skills they can use on their own. Like real skills, real workflows, real automation. You type one thing, the AI figures out what to do and it just does it. No babysitting, no repeating yourself, no micromanaging. And the new update makes it even more insane because now the AI doesn't just wait for instructions. It anticipates what you need and acts on it automatically. For anyone building a business with AI right now, this is the one to watch. By the end of this video, you're going to see exactly how it works, why it matters, and how you can start using it today. Let's get into it. Hey, if we haven't met already, I'm the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of SEO agency Goldie Agency. Whilst he's helping clients get more leads and customers, I'm here to help you get the latest AI updates. Julian Goldie reads every comment, so make sure you comment below. All right, let's talk about anti-gravity because this update just changed the game in a big way, and I want to make sure you understand exactly what this means for you, your business, and how you work with AI going forward. So, let's start from the beginning. What actually is anti-gravity? It's a development environment, but not like the ones you've used before. Most AI tools work like this. You type something, the AI suggests something, you copy it, you move on. That's it. That's the whole thing. Anti-gravity is built completely differently. It's built around AI agents. And agents don't just suggest stuff. They actually do the work. They plan features. They write code. They run tests. They find bugs. They fix them. They ship all on their own while you watch and supervise. You're not writing every line anymore. You're giving goals and managing AI workers who execute them. That's a completely different way of building and it supports big powerful models too. Gemini 3 Pro, Gemini Flash, Gemini Deep Think, Claude Sun and GPT models all inside one tool. That alone makes it one of the most powerful environments available right now. But the update everyone is losing their minds over, that's the new skill system, and that's what we're really here to talk about. Here's what just dropped. Anti-gravity now has something called agent skills. And this is where it gets really exciting. A skill is basically a reusable workflow for an AI agent. Think of it like a playbook or a standard operating procedure, but instead of a human following it, the AI follows it. You teach the AI how to do something once. It remembers it. From that point on, it just uses it automatically whenever you need it. Before this update, you had to repeat yourself constantly every single chat, every single session. Use this tone, right? Follow this format. check for these issues over and over again. It was exhausting and inconsistent. Now, you build a skill, the AI learns it, and it applies it perfectly every single time without you saying a word. That is a massive upgrade. And once you see how it works in practice, you're going to want to start building skills immediately. Actually, before I go deeper on this, let me quickly mention something. If you want to learn exactly how to use tools like anti-gravity to automate your business, save hours every week, and build smarter AI systems, come join us in the AI Profit Boardroom. It's where we break down practical AI automation in a way that actually makes sense. No fluff, no jargon, just real strategies you can use right now. Link is in the description. Go check it out and then come back because this next part is really important. Okay, so here is how skills actually get triggered. There are two ways. The first way is called implicit skill calling. This is the automatic one. You don't tell the AI which skill to use. It figures it out by itself based on what you're asking for. So, let's say you're working on something like the AI profit boardroom and you type this prompt. Check if the AI profit boardroom landing page is loading correctly and flag anything that might be hurting conversions. The agent reads that. It understands the intent. It automatically loads the right skill for the job, maybe a site health checker or a performance audit workflow, and it runs it. You didn't pick the skill. You didn't even mention it. The agent just knew. That is called semantic skill discovery. The AI matches the meaning of your words to the right skill automatically. The second way is called explicit skill calling. This is the manual one. You type the skill name directly with a slash in front of it. Something like code review or or deploy app or content audit. Instant direct total control. You know exactly what's running and when. So you get the best of both worlds. Full automation when you want speed. Manual control when you need precision. That combination is really smart because it works for beginners and advanced users at the same time. Let me make this practical. Let's say you're running a business like the AI Profit Boardroom. You've got landing pages. You've got email sequences. You've got content going out every single week. You've got community members joining and on boarding. Here's how you'd actually use anti-gravity skills in that context. You build a skill that knows exactly how to audit your landing pages. It checks for weak headlines, confusing copy, missing calls to action, slow loading elements, anything that could be stopping people from joining. Then, whenever you update a page, you just say, "Review the AI profit boardroom homepage and tell me what's stopping people from clicking the join button." The agent loads the skill and runs a full audit. No extra instructions needed. Content generator skill. You build a skill that knows your brand voice. It knows your audience. It knows what the AI profit boardroom is about, who it's for, and what problems it solves. Then every time you need content, you just give it a direction. So, how does a skill actually work? Each skill lives inside a folder. Inside that folder is a file called skilled empty. That one file tells the AI everything it needs to know, what the skill is called, what it does, when to use it. The exact step-by-step instructions for completing it. When anti-gravity starts up, it scans all your skill folders. It reads the names and descriptions. It stores them as available capabilities. Then when you send a prompt, the AI runs a match between what you said and what skills are available and it loads the right one. There are two types of skill locations. Workspace skills live inside your project folder and are specific to that one project. So your AI profit boardroom landing page review skill lives inside that project only. Global skills live in your main settings and work across every single project you run. So something like a general content style guide would be global. You build it once and it applies everywhere. Because the agent only loads the skill it actually needs. It keeps everything lean and fast. Low token usage, high reliability. The system is smart enough to know what to grab and what to leave alone. Here's the progression of where we've been with AI. First, it was you doing everything and AI watching. Then AI started suggesting things and you deciding. Now AI is doing the work and you supervising. That is a massive jump and skills accelerate that jump significantly because now the AI isn't just smart in the moment. It's trained. It knows your systems. It knows your standards. It knows your voice. It knows what good looks like for your business specifically. It becomes a real teammate. Not a chatbot you have to reexplain everything to every single time. And here's the bigger trend worth paying attention to. Other platforms are adopting the same skill standard. Cursor is doing it. Anthropic is building toward it. other AI coding environments are following, which means skills you build now could be transferable across tools in the future. You're not just [snorts] building for anti-gravity. You're building a reusable library of AI capabilities for your entire business. That's real long-term leverage. So that's the anti-gravity skills update. AI agents that learn your workflows, apply them automatically, and give you full control when you need it. This is the transition from AI assistants to AI workers, and it's happening right now.
